---
title: "Is jank fast yet?"
execute:
  echo: false
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
jupyter: python3
---

This page monitors relative performance of Clojure implementations over time.

[github.com:kylc/is-jank-fast-yet](https://github.com/kylc/is-jank-fast-yet)

```{python}
import os
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
```

```{python}
# | output: asis

DATA_DIR = Path(os.environ.get("IJFY_DATA_DIR", "data"))

bench_file_paths = list(DATA_DIR.glob("*.csv"))
dfs = [
    pd.read_csv(p, names=["start_time", "language", "version", "duration_ms"])
    for p in bench_file_paths
]

# Compute a common X range for all plots.
xmin = min(df["start_time"].min() for df in dfs)
xmax = max(df["start_time"].max() for df in dfs)

for bench_file_path, df in zip(bench_file_paths, dfs):
    print(f"### {bench_file_path.stem}")

    fig = px.line(
        df,
        x="start_time",
        y="duration_ms",
        range_x=[xmin, xmax],
        color="language",
        template="plotly_white",
        hover_name="language",
        hover_data=["start_time", "version", "duration_ms"],
        labels={
            "start_time": "Run Date (UTC)",
            "duration_ms": "Execution Time (ms)",
            "language": "Language",
            "version": "Version",
        },
        markers=True,
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        legend=dict(
            orientation="h",
            yanchor="bottom",
            y=1.02,
            xanchor="right",
            x=1,
            title_text="Language",
        )
    )
    fig.show(config={"displayModeBar": False})
```
